title: myristicaceae
intro: The myristicaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, specifically a group of evergreen trees and shrubs that are primarily found in tropical regions. Myristicaceae is characterized by its unique combination of features, including aromatic fruits and seeds, and a distinctive, often resinous bark. This family comprises approximately 100 species, with many species being economically important for their edible fruits and seeds. Examples of plants in the myristicaceae family include the nutmeg tree (Myristica fragrans) and the nutmeg nut (Dacryocarpus exasperatus).
